name: Lyon Arboretum
tagline: A 194-acre rainforest research garden in upper Manoa Valley — quiet trails, rare tropical plants, and the feel of deep jungle.
parking: Limited lot; arrive early or carpool
bestTime: Weekday mornings for solitude; expect afternoon showers
talkStory: Operated by the University of Hawaii, this 194-acre botanical garden in upper Manoa Valley feels like a secret rainforest just minutes from the city. The trails wind through rare tropical plant collections, native Hawaiian species, and groves of towering trees. Far less crowded than nearby Manoa Falls, it offers a more immersive jungle experience. Reservations required — book online in advance.
